message:
test(tui_web_dashboard): correct copy-pasted expect message on read paths

Three `serde_json::from_str(...).expect("write failed")` sites were
reading JSON the test had just produced and asserting on its
contents — not writing. The "write failed" message was almost
certainly copy-pasted from an earlier write-oriented test and would
have been actively misleading if the deserialization ever actually
panicked (readers would hunt for a broken write path that does not
exist).

Change the expect message to "valid json" in:
  - `cached_snapshot` payload check after `snapshot_seq() == 1`
  - `cached_snapshot` check for keys with backslashes / quotes /
    newlines in `ops\qa` screen naming
  - `handle_inactive_input_returns_503` 503 payload check

No assertions changed; behaviour-equivalent refactor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
